paper_title: The ATIS Spoken Language Systems Pilot Corpus
evaluation_method: The ATIS (Airline Travel Information Systems) corpus was created by Hemphill, Godfrey and Doddington (1990) from transcribed audio recordings of interactions between human users and a flight scheduling system. The dataset has 26 intent categories and 83 slot types. Two primary tasks are evaluated: (1) Intent Detection (ID): classification accuracy measuring the proportion of utterances whose intent is correctly predicted. (2) Slot Filling (SF): F1 score computed using the CoNLL evaluation script, where a slot is correct only if both its range and type match the gold label exactly.
